title: Anil amorphoides
taxonomy: Anil amorphoides is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is divided into approximately 600 genera and over 13,000 species. Anil amorphoides is part of the Anil genus, which is native to tropical Asia. The classification of Anil amorphoides is further detailed within the subfamily Rubioideae.

intro: Anil amorphoides, also known as Anil or Pushter, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antipyretic effects, making it useful in treating fever, headache, and digestive disorders. The bioactive constituents of Anil amorphoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
